zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Pearle於1584年出生在英國賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯時，她的父親William Justice Pearle20歲，母親Matilda Sarah Robinson20歲。她於1609年與Ebenezer James Peabody在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1660年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加羅德 易曼德紐波特享年76歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Pearle于1584年出生在英国賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯时，她的父亲William Justice Pearle20岁，母亲Matilda Sarah Robinson20岁。她于1609年与Ebenezer James Peabody在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1660年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加羅德 易曼德紐波特享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: Pearle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh, English, and Irish: variant of   Pierce  .

variants.name: Pierce
meanings: English, Welsh, and Irish: from the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal name Piers, Peres, Peris, an Old French nominative of Pierre or Per, the French form of Peter . Pierce is especially frequent in northwestern Wales and Lancashire, while Pearce is the more common spelling in the rest of England. In Ireland Pierce and Pearse represent a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Piarais ‘son of Piaras’, a Gaelicized form of Piers.
Americanized form of some similar-sounding Jewish surname.
History: Franklin Pierce (1804–69), 14th president of the US, was born in Hillsborough, NH, on the New England frontier. His English ancestor Thomas Pierce emigrated to Charlestown, MA, in 1633/34.

variants.name: Peers
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gabrielle, Girard.
North German and Flemish: patronymic from the personal name   Peer  .

variants.name: Parrott
meanings: English: from a short form of the Middle English personal name Peres, Peris (from Old French Pierre; see Pearce ) + the diminutive suffixes -et, -ot.
English: habitational name from North Perrott in Somerset or South Perrott in Dorset (both named from the River Parret on which they stand, the etymology of which name is uncertain).
Altered form of French Parrot or of its variant Parot or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname, reflecting the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.

variants.name: Parlett
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Perlet, Parlet, a pet form of Peres (i.e. Peter, see Pearce ) + the double diminutive suffix -elet. For the variation in the main vowel compare Parkin and Perkin .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: Providence Founded
historicEvents.description: In 1636 Providence is founded as a settlement for Rhode Island by Roger Williams

historicEvents.title: First Baptist Church
historicEvents.description: Roger Williams establishes the First Baptist Church of Rhode Island in Providence during 1638.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Pearle was born in 1584, in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England, her father, William Justice Pearle, was 20 and her mother, Matilda Sarah Robinson, was 20. She married Ebenezer James Peabody in 1609, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1660, in Newport, Rhode Island, British Colonial America, at the age of 76.

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Pearle(이)가 1584년 영국 허트퍼드실르 스트 알반스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Justice Pearle(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda Sarah Robinson(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1609년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Ebenezer James Peabody(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1660년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 호드 이슬랜드 뉴포트에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Pearle родилась в 1584 г. в местности ст албанс, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Justice Pearle было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda Sarah Robinson, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ebenezer James Peabody в 1609 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1660 г. в местности нюпорт, род исланд, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 76 лет.
